title: Pilot: synthetic survival-analysis cohort (data file)
context: The M0 pilot proves the end-to-end production pipeline on a synthetic dataset. A synthetic survival-analysis cohort (stage, treatment arm, censored survival times) is the chosen pilot because: (1) survival analysis (Kaplan-Meier, Cox models) is a core cancer data-science skill; (2) purely synthetic generation from published aggregate survival statistics keeps it riskTier:low with re-identification impossible; (3) the self-serve fallback (Zenodo DOI + training-repo PR) means a real reusable outcome exists even before a confirmed partner. This task produces the data file only; the teaching layer is pilot-lesson-009. Depends on all M0 foundation tasks: template-001 (canonical model), gate-002 (eligibility gate), synthesis-004 (generation methodology), tooling-006 (generator harness), and reviewer-005 (safety reviewer named).
objective: Produce the synthetic survival-analysis cohort data file end-to-end using the project generator harness, pass the eligibility/safety gate, complete documentation to >= 90/100 completeness, mint a Zenodo DOI, and publish via self-serve fallback or confirmed partner.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated by the harness from a committed seed + parameter file, each parameter provenanced to a published aggregate survival/stage source (citation + value).
Passed gate-002 (synthetic; no controlled-access lineage; safety notice present) with the PASS artifact committed; riskTier:low.

Fields documented (data dictionary): patient_id (synthetic), stage, treatment_arm, time_to_event, event_observed (censoring), and any additional fields; clinically plausible censoring + survival distributions.
Reproducibility check = PASS: independent reviewer re-runs seed/params and produces identical file.
Documentation completeness score >= 90/100; Croissant metadata valid; Zenodo DOI minted; outputLicense: CC0-1.0 (or CC-BY-4.0 if calibrated from a CC-BY source, attributed).

Published via Zenodo + a training-repo PR (self-serve fallback) and, if a channel exists, adopted with the steward's outcomes/<dataset-id>.json evidence recorded — else published with the adoption blocker surfaced.
Safety reviewer (reviewer-005) has reviewed and signed off on the gate artifact.
resources: SEER/NCI aggregate cancer survival statistics (candidate calibration source)
output: A synthetic survival-analysis cohort CSV/TSV data file with provenance.json, gate PASS artifact, Croissant metadata, data dictionary, and Zenodo DOI — the M0 pilot dataset proving the end-to-end production pipeline.
